title: Miscellaneous Instructional Policies: Teaching About Controversial Issues
description: Teachers and staff follow clear guidelines when discussing controversial or sensitive topics in the classroom, ensuring that instruction remains balanced and age-appropriate. The policy helps protect students' rights to form their own opinions while making sure no single political or ideological viewpoint is promoted over others. Parents and community members can trust that classroom discussions on complex issues are handled thoughtfully and in line with district standards.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD Policy EMB significantly expands the scope of the "Teaching About Controversial Issues" policy by replacing references to "teachers" with the broader term "Subject Persons" (defined to include employees, volunteers, and contractors), and adds numerous new sections aligned with Texas S.B. 12 (89th Legislature) covering holidays/commemorative months, human sexuality instruction with written parental consent requirements, pornographic material prohibition, diversity/equity/inclusion restrictions, gender identity and sexual orientation prohibitions, social transitioning prohibition, prohibited terms, student First Amendment rights, and superintendent compliance certification.

This update to Conroe ISD's EMB (Local) policy on Teaching About Controversial Issues significantly expands the scope of persons covered (from "Teachers" to "Subject Persons"), adds new sections on holidays/commemorative months, human sexuality instruction with parental consent requirements, pornographic material prohibition, diversity/equity/inclusion restrictions, social transitioning, prohibited terms, student First Amendment rights, discipline for violations, policy distribution, and superintendent authority — all aligned with Texas legislation from the 87th, 88th, and 89th legislative sessions, including S.B. 12 (89th Legislature).

compliance_assessment.analysis: This EMB (Local) policy update draws from multiple Texas legislative mandates across three legislative sessions, but also contains locally chosen structural and procedural elements that exceed what any single statute requires. Several provisions are Required by Law: the human sexuality instruction parental consent requirements implement TEC §28.004 (as amended through the 87th and 88th sessions); the pornographic material prohibition aligns with TEC §43.24 and S.B. 12 (89th Leg.), which restricts sexually explicit materials in schools; the social transitioning restrictions and related prohibited terms provisions reflect H.B. 900 (88th Leg.) and S.B. 12 (89th Leg.) mandates; and the diversity/equity/inclusion restrictions track legislative limitations imposed through rider provisions and potentially H.B. 3979/S.B. 3 (87th Leg., relating to civics and controversial topics instruction under TEC §28.002). However, several elements are Locally Discretionary and reflect Conroe ISD's own policy choices beyond what the law specifically mandates: (1) the expansion of scope from 'Teachers' to 'Subject Persons,' which is a local drafting choice broader than any statutory definition; (2) the Holidays/Commemorative Months section, which no specific Texas statute requires to appear in this policy; (3) the specific superintendent authority and compliance certification mechanism created locally; (4) the policy distribution requirement detailing how the policy is disseminated; (5) the specific discipline framework for violations of this policy, which goes beyond what the cited statutes prescribe in terms of disciplinary consequences; and (6) the student First Amendment rights section, which, while referencing constitutional doctrine, represents a locally chosen articulation not mandated by a specific statute. Because the policy contains both legally compelled elements tracking specific Texas statutes and bills, and locally tailored additions that reflect district discretion, the appropriate classification is Mixed.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's EMB Local Policy substantially expands the Teaching About Controversial Issues policy by adding new sections on: the definition of "Subject Persons," prohibitions on teaching Critical Race Theory and CRT/SDI ideologies, restrictions on divisive Social and Emotional Learning (SEL) concepts, a new Gender Fluidity prohibition, and a Pronoun Usage section — all grounded in legislation from the 87th, 88th, and 89th Texas legislative sessions. The update also revises the Selection of Topics and Classroom Discussion sections to align with updated Texas law requirements.
timeline_local.changes_added:
["As used in this policy, "Subject Persons" means those persons who (1) are employed by the District, (2) volunteer time or services on behalf of the District or and (3) directly interact with District students.",
"Teachers shall not be compelled to discuss widely debated and currently controversial issues of public policy or social affairs. However, in the event a teacher chooses to discuss a topic described above, the teacher must explore that topic objectively and in a manner free from political bias. In guiding classroom discussion of controversial issues, teachers shall comply with the instructional requirements and prohibitions imposed under Texas law and by the District.",
"5. Avoid any attempt to coerce or persuade students to adopt the teacher's point of view.",
"5. 6. Comply with the instructional requirements and prohibitions imposed under Texas law.",
"Certain Instructional Requirements and Prohibitions [section heading]",
"The District, including its teachers and administrators, shall not:",
"1. Require, teach, make part of a course or training, or otherwise instruct any Subject Persons or students that: a. One race or sex is inherently superior to another race or sex; b. An individual, by virtue of the individual's race or sex, is inherently racist, sexist, or oppressive, whether consciously or unconsciously; c. An individual should be discriminated against or receive adverse treatment solely or partly because of the individual's race or sex; d. An individual's moral character, standing, or worth is partially or necessarily determined by the individual's race or sex; e. An individual, by virtue of the individual's race or sex, bears responsibility, blame, or guilt for actions committed by other members of the same race or sex; f. Meritocracy or traits such as a hard work ethic are racist or sexist or were created by members of a particular race or group to oppress members of another race or group; g. The delivery of slaves and the advent of slavery in the territory that is now the United States constituted the true founding of the United States; or h. With respect to their relationship to American values, slavery and racism are anything other than deviations from, betrayals of, or failures to live up to the authentic founding principles of the United States, which include liberty and equality;",
"2. Teach, instruct, train, introduce, discuss or require an understanding of The 1619 Project, which began as a compilation of essays, poems, and other works originally published in The New York Times Magazine in August 2019 but has become the impetus for the development of resources, programs, and curricula for educators; or",
"3. Teach, instruct, advocate, promote, or discuss any ideas, beliefs, concepts, theories, principles, rules, thoughts, or impressions that have any connection to, relationship with, refer to, are influenced by, or are otherwise consistent with so-called "Critical Race Theory" or systemic discrimination ideologies, including, but not limited to, those ideologies set forth in this section of this policy, as such ideologies have been understood, developed, taught, interpreted, defined/redefined by individuals, organizations, and others who have created, defined, redefined, interpreted, taught, instructed, advocated, promoted, or discussed or used to understand so-called "Critical Race Theory" or systemic discrimination ideologies. This subsection, along with subsection (1) and (2) above, shall be collectively referred to as "Critical Race Theory or Systemic Discrimination Ideologies" or "CRT/SDI'".",
"Social and Emotional Learning Concepts [section heading]",
"Most traditional social and emotional learning (SEL) teachings are consistent with the District's general education goals, particularly concepts relating to the development of self-awareness, individualism, self-reliance, self-motivation, communication, conflict resolution, and interpersonal skills that are vital for academic, professional, and life success.",
"However, some SEL concepts can be potentially divisive, conflict with District policy, and be inconsistent with the District's education goals. As such, the District, including its Subject Persons, shall not teach, instruct, train or otherwise require any Subject Person or any individual or group, to adopt, support, or otherwise promote SEL concepts that are divisive, conflict with District policy, or are inconsistent with the District's education goals.",
"For the avoidance of doubt, the District will continue to support and promote the following ideologies and concepts, which are generally consistent with the positive components of SEL and with the general educational goals of the state and District: individualism; a rejection of victimhood mentality; conflict resolution techniques; aspiration to serve as entrepreneurs and spiritual and community leaders; financial self-sufficiency; importance of the nuclear family; liberty, hard work and perseverance as the basis for a successful society; and the virtues of self-discipline, forgiveness, patience, kindness, determination, hope, thankfulness, reliability, honesty, industry and responsibility.",
"Gender Fluidity [section heading]",
"The District, including its Subject Persons, shall not teach, instruct, train or otherwise require any Subject Person or any individual or group, to adopt, support or otherwise promote Gender Fluidity (as defined herein). Additionally, Subject Persons shall not teach, instruct, train, or otherwise require any other Subject Person to teach, instruct, train, or otherwise communicate to any individual or group topics regarding sexual orientation or gender identity.",
"For purposes of this policy, "Gender Fluidity" means any belief, theory or ideology that: 1. Espouses the view that gender is merely a social construct; 2. Espouses the view that it is possible for a person to be any gender or none (i.e., non-binary); 3. Espouses the view that an individual's biological sex should attempt to be changed to "match" a gender different from that person's biological sex; and 4. Supports hormone therapy or other medical treatments or procedures to temporarily or permanently alter a person's body so that it "matches" a gender different from that person's biological sex.",
"A Subject Person may not use, or permit others to use, any District resource, funds, property, or personnel to adopt, support, or promote Gender Fluidity as defined herein.",
"Pronoun Usage [section heading]",
"The District will not promote, require or encourage the use of pronoun identifiers for students, teachers or any other persons in any manner that is inconsistent with the biological sex of such person as listed on: 1. The student's official birth certificate; or 2. If the student's official birth certificate is unobtainable, another government-issued record.",
"However, to the extent a student (with the written consent of such student's parent or legal guardian), parent or legal guardian has specifically, in writing, requested or directed the use of a specific pronoun for that particular student, a teacher or administrator interacting with the student may comply with such request.",
"A Subject Person shall not be permitted to require or request a student to use a pronoun to refer to such Subject Person in a manner that is inconsistent with the biological sex of such Subject Person as listed on: 1. The Subject Person's official birth certificate; or 2. If the Subject Person's official birth certificate is unobtainable, another government-issued record.",
"A parent of a student enrolled in the district or a district employee may report to the board of trustees a suspected violation of this gender fluidity and/or pronoun usage policy. The board shall investigate any suspected violation and determine whether the violation occurred. If the board determines that a district employee has violated this policy, the board shall immediately report the violation to the commissioner."]
changes_removed: A teacher selecting topics for discussion in the classroom shall be adequately informed about the issue and capable of providing instruction on the subject, free from personal bias.

compliance_assessment.analysis: Several elements of this EMB Local update directly implement Texas statutory requirements from the 87th and 88th Legislative Sessions. The 'Certain Instructional Requirements and Prohibitions' section (items 1a–1h) mirrors the CRT-related prohibitions enacted in H.B. 3979 (87th Leg.) and S.B. 3 (87th Leg., 2nd Called Session), codified at TEC §28.002 and TEC §28.0022, which prohibit teaching the enumerated race/sex-based concepts in Texas public schools. The teacher non-compulsion language ('Teachers shall not be compelled to discuss widely debated and currently controversial issues') also tracks TEC §28.002(h-2) as amended. The pronoun usage section restricting non-biological-sex-consistent pronoun use and tying it to birth certificate documentation reflects requirements imposed by S.B. 12 or related legislation from the 88th or 89th Legislative Sessions addressing gender identity in schools, and the board reporting/commissioner notification mechanism for pronoun violations tracks statutory enforcement obligations. However, several elements reflect locally chosen additions that go significantly beyond what any specific statute mandates: (1) the 'Subject Persons' definition, which extends policy coverage beyond employees to volunteers who interact with students — a locally crafted scope expansion; (2) the entire 'Social and Emotional Learning Concepts' section, including both the prohibition on 'divisive' SEL and the affirmative list of approved SEL values (individualism, rejection of victimhood mentality, nuclear family, entrepreneurship, etc.) — no Texas statute prescribes this specific framework or approved-values list; (3) the locally defined term 'Critical Race Theory or Systemic Discrimination Ideologies' (CRT/SDI), which expands the statutory prohibitions to a locally coined and broadly defined category going well beyond TEC §28.0022's enumerated items; (4) the 'Gender Fluidity' section's four-part local definition of 'Gender Fluidity,' which is a locally drafted construct not mandated by statute in that specific form; (5) the prohibition on Subject Persons using or permitting others to use district resources to promote Gender Fluidity — a locally tailored enforcement mechanism; (6) the parental written-consent exception allowing teachers to optionally comply with non-biological pronoun requests — a discretionary local accommodation mechanism; and (7) the direct board-to-commissioner reporting obligation for gender fluidity/pronoun violations, which constitutes a locally structured enforcement escalation process beyond what statute specifically prescribes in this procedural form.

description_local: This policy sets guidelines for how controversial or sensitive topics must be handled during classroom instruction. It applies to a broader group of school district employees — not just classroom teachers, but potentially other staff involved in instruction or educational activities. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it shapes how schools approach discussions of complex or contested subjects, helping ensure that multiple perspectives are considered and that students are not unduly influenced by the personal views of school staff.
